titleEn: Panera Bread sued over meat marketing claims
titleZh: Panera Bread因肉类营销声明被起诉
summaryZh: 一家食品安全与动物福利非营利组织起诉Panera Bread，指控其误导消费者关于肉类产品的动物福利标准。诉讼引用2024年路透社报道，称该公司为节省约2100万美元以筹备IPO，放宽了原料标准，并撤销了此前承诺的无抗生素和人性化采购政策。Panera去年报告称其鸡肉供应链中无一家供应商符合其自身标准。该事件凸显了食品行业在动物福利承诺与商业利益之间的冲突，对畜牧供应链的透明度与诚信提出挑战。
featuredReason: 该新闻揭示了大型餐饮品牌在动物福利承诺与商业利益之间的博弈，对畜牧行业供应链的诚信与透明度具有警示意义，值得关注消费者信任与市场合规的从业者阅读。
contentFull: Company News Panera Bread sued over meat marketing claims Nonprofit alleges misleading animal welfare standards 27 March 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media North America A food safety and animal welfare nonprofit filed suit against Panera Bread last week based on Reuters reporting, claiming the company misled consumers about Panera’s meat products, reported Reuters.Food Animal Concerns Trust filed suit against the fast casual chain on March 20 in a local court in Washington D.C., citing Reuters reporting in 2024 on internal company documents about how the company loosened ingredient standards to save an estimated $21 million ahead of a planned IPO.The lawsuit claims Panera Bread positioned itself as a leader on antibiotic-free ad humane sourcing for years before withdrawing those policies while continuing to market itself on those grounds. The lawsuit cites among others Panera's decade-old commitment to eventually use chickens from suppliers that provide sufficient living space to the livestock. Panera reported last year that none of its chicken supply came from suppliers that meet the company's standard.Panera Bread's chief corporate affairs officer Brooke Buchanan said in a statement: "We take animal welfare seriously at Panera and our beliefs on animal health and welfare are clearly stated on our website."Based near St. Louis, Panera has more than 2,000 stores in the U.S. and Canada. It was acquired and taken private in 2017 for $7.5 billion by Luxembourg-headquartered private equity firm JAB Holdings. In late 2023, the company filed for an initial public offering, though it remains private. translationZh:
一家食品安全与动物福利非营利组织上周根据路透社的报道对Panera Bread提起诉讼，声称该公司在肉类产品方面误导了消费者，据路透社报道。

食品动物关注信托基金于3月20日在华盛顿特区当地法院对这家快餐连锁店提起诉讼，引用了路透社2024年关于公司内部文件的报道，这些文件显示该公司为在计划IPO前节省约2100万美元而放宽了原料标准。

该诉讼称，Panera Bread多年来将自己定位为无抗生素和人性化采购的领导者，但在撤销这些政策后仍继续以此为基础进行营销。诉讼特别提到了Panera十年前的一项承诺，即最终从为家畜提供足够生活空间的供应商处采购鸡肉。Panera去年报告称，其鸡肉供应中没有一家来自符合公司标准的供应商。

Panera Bread首席企业事务官Brooke Buchanan在一份声明中表示：“我们在Panera非常重视动物福利，我们在动物健康与福利方面的立场已明确列在我们的网站上。”

Panera总部位于圣路易斯附近，在美国和加拿大拥有超过2000家门店。2017年，它被总部位于卢森堡的私募股权公司JAB Holdings以75亿美元收购并私有化。2023年底，该公司申请了首次公开募股，但目前仍为私有状态。
contentHtml:
Panera Bread sued over meat marketing claims 
Nonprofit alleges misleading animal welfare standards
